data.title: Insurance Firms Develop New Policies Amid Rising Threats to Cryptocurrency Holders
data.description: According to PANews, a surge in violent attacks targeting cryptocurrency holders is prompting insurance companies to introduce new protective measures. Recent incidents, such as the prolonged detention and torture of an Italian tourist in New York and an attempted kidnapping involving the family of a French cryptocurrency executive, highlight the tangible security threats facing the crypto industry. At least three companies specializing in crypto insurance and security are reportedly preparing customized kidnap and ransom (K&R) insurance policies for digital asset investors.

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin Spot ETFs Hold Over 1.2 Million BTC
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from HODL15Capital reveals that as of today, U.S. Bitcoin spot ETFs collectively hold 1,205,626 BTC. BlackRock's IBIT leads with 660,814 BTC, followed by Fidelity's FBTC with 198,159 BTC and Grayscale's GBTC with 186,622 BTC.

data.title: The risk of kidnapping in the crypto industry is rising, and insurance companies are accelerating the launch of customized kidnapping and ransom policies
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Recent violent kidnappings of cryptocurrency holders have prompted insurance companies to accelerate the launch of kidnapping and ransom (K&R) insurance products designed specifically for digital asset investors. It is reported that companies such as AnchorWatch, Relm Insurance and Hylant Capital are developing customized policies covering kidnapping, extortion and crisis response. Rebecca Rubenfeld, chief operating officer of AnchorWatch, said that at the Bitcoin Conference held in Las Vegas, security issues became the focus of participants. Joseph Ziolkowski, CEO of Relm Insurance, pointed out that premium pricing will be evaluated based on the customer's physical and network security measures. (Cointelegraph)
